[[JA:水主神社 (城陽市)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153262}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Mizushi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto.jpg|250px]]
Place of worship (honden in the back) |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Joyo|Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto}}
Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major)
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建 = Reign of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday in mid-November |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mizushi Shrine|position=top|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=180|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mizushi Shrine''' (みずしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} enshrined at Mizushi Miyababa, {{jalink|Joyo|Q817276}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|major shikinaisha|Q134917288}}, and its former rank was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === According to shrine tradition, the ten deities currently enshrined are as follows: *{{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami}} *{{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|Q106241464|Amemurakumo-no-Kami}} *{{jalink|Ame-no-Oshio-no-Mikoto|Q97613650|Ame-no-Oshio-no-Kami}} *Take-Nukashi-Aka-no-Mikoto *Take-Tsutsukusa-no-Mikoto *Take-Tase-no-Mikoto *Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto *Yamatotama-hiko-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto However, according to the "National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals," the enshrined deities are the following five: *Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami *Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto *Oonui-no-Mikoto *Konui-no-Mikoto According to the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}, the entry indicates ten seats at Mizushi Shrine and notes participation of two deities in the {{jalink|Ainame Festival|Q11581944}}, showing that there were originally ten deities, two of which were Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto. === History === === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, in the reign of Emperor Sujin, Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto moved {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} to Yamato Kasanui no Mura, and Nunakiiri-hime-no-Mikoto was made to enshrine Yamato Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamato and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamashiro; this shrine is identified with the Yamashiro enshrinement. Since ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362}}'' ({{jalink|815|Q29917}}) states "Yamashiro no kuni shinbetsu, {{jalink|Mizushi clan||Mizushi no Atai}}, descendants of Hoakari-no-Mikoto," it is inferred that this shrine originally enshrined the clan ancestor {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Ame-no-Hoakari Myojin}} (Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami). === Overview === In {{jalink|858|Q30371}} ({{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2) rain-prayer rites were held, and in {{jalink|859|Q23897}} ({{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 1) prayers for wind and rain were performed. National histories repeatedly record offerings for rain prayer at this shrine, and in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Rinji-sai|Q11612499}} it was selected among the eighty-five rain-prayer deities, indicating a strong reputation for efficacy in rain prayer. In the Engishiki Jinmyocho it is ranked as a major shrine. === Divine ranks === *{{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" *{{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 8 ({{jalink|866|Q23872}}) {{jalink|November 20|Q3024}}, promoted from Junior Fourth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Upper (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" === Precincts === The area beyond the worship hall is protected by iron bars; since the main hall and precinct shrines are all inside, visitors cannot approach directly in front of the main hall. The Mizushi Shrine main hall stands in front, with Kabaitsuki Shrine (below) on the left and Koromonui Shrine on the right. ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Honden.jpg|Main hall ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Haiden.jpg|Worship hall ファイル:Koromonui Jinja Mizushi.jpg|Koromonui Shrine === Kabaitsuki Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Kabaitsuki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Kabaitsuki Jinja Mizushi.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Joyo|Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{Coord|34|51|17|N|135|47|22.4|E|region:JP-26|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Tsukuyomi|Q595520|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|February 20|Q2344}} |神事 = |地図 = }} '''Kabaitsuki Shrine''' (かばいづきじんじゃ) is a precinct shrine of Mizushi Shrine. It is a {{jalink|major shikinaisha|Q134917288}}. ==== History ==== Its founding date is unknown. In the same {{jalink|Tsuzuki District|Q1154678}}, there is the shikinaisha {{jalink|Tsukuyomi Shrine (Kyotanabe)|Q54153263|Tsukuyomi Shrine}}, and a relationship has been pointed out. It is also said that this shrine formerly stood in Osumi together with Tsukuyomi Shrine. However, due to flooding of the {{jalink|Kizu River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|Kizu River}}, the shrine buildings were washed away, and in {{jalink|1672|Q7614}} ({{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 12) it was relocated into the precincts of Mizushi Shrine. Afterward it came under shared administration with Mizushi Shrine. ==== Divine rank ==== *{{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabaitsuki no Kami" === Site information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Joyo|Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085|Kinki Nippon Railway}} {{jalink|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|Kyoto Line}} {{jalink|Tonominosho Station|Q7821630}}; then 20 minutes on foot westward === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みすししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}, and its former rank was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === According to shrine tradition, the ten deities currently enshrined are as follows: *{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami}} *{{ill||lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|Q106241464|WD=Q106241464|lt=Amemurakumo-no-Kami}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Mikoto|Q97613650|WD=Q97613650|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Kami}} *Take-Nukashi-Aka-no-Mikoto *Take-Tsutsukusa-no-Mikoto *Take-Tase-no-Mikoto *Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto *Yamatotama-hiko-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto However, according to the "National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals," the enshrined deities are the following five: *Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami *Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto *Oonui-no-Mikoto *Konui-no-Mikoto According to the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, the entry indicates ten seats at Mizushi Shrine and notes participation of two deities in the {{ill|lt=Ainame Festival|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944}}, showing that there were originally ten deities, two of which were Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto. === History === === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, in the reign of Emperor Sujin, Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto moved {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} to Yamato Kasanui no Mura, and Nunakiiri-hime-no-Mikoto was made to enshrine Yamato Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamato and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamashiro; this shrine is identified with the Yamashiro enshrinement. Since ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' ({{ill|lt=815|Q29917|WD=Q29917}}) states "Yamashiro no kuni shinbetsu, {{ill|lt=Mizushi clan||WD=|lt=Mizushi no Atai}}, descendants of Hoakari-no-Mikoto," it is inferred that this shrine originally enshrined the clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari Myojin}} (Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami). === Overview === In {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2) rain-prayer rites were held, and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 1) prayers for wind and rain were performed. National histories repeatedly record offerings for rain prayer at this shrine, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Rinji-sai|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499}} it was selected among the eighty-five rain-prayer deities, indicating a strong reputation for efficacy in rain prayer. In the Engishiki Jinmyocho it is ranked as a major shrine. === Divine ranks === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" *{{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872}}) {{ill|lt=November 20|Q3024|WD=Q3024}}, promoted from Junior Fourth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Upper (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" === Precincts === The area beyond the worship hall is protected by iron bars; since the main hall and precinct shrines are all inside, visitors cannot approach directly in front of the main hall. The Mizushi Shrine main hall stands in front, with Kabaitsuki Shrine (below) on the left and Koromonui Shrine on the right. ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Honden.jpg|Main hall ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Haiden.jpg|Worship hall ファイル:Koromonui Jinja Mizushi.jpg|Koromonui Shrine === Kabaitsuki Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Kabaitsuki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Kabaitsuki Jinja Mizushi.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{Coord|34|51|17|N|135|47|22.4|E|region:JP-26|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (major) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=February 20|Q2344|WD=Q2344}} |神事 = |地図 = }} '''Kabaitsuki Shrine''' (かばいづきじんじゃ) is a precinct shrine of Mizushi Shrine. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}. ==== History ==== Its founding date is unknown. In the same {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678}}, there is the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine (Kyotanabe)|Q54153263|WD=Q54153263|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine}}, and a relationship has been pointed out. It is also said that this shrine formerly stood in Osumi together with Tsukuyomi Shrine. However, due to flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kizu River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River}}, the shrine buildings were washed away, and in {{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 12) it was relocated into the precincts of Mizushi Shrine. Afterward it came under shared administration with Mizushi Shrine. ==== Divine rank ==== *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabaitsuki no Kami" === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kinki Nippon Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kyoto Line}} {{ill|lt=Tonominosho Station|Q7821630|WD=Q7821630}}; then 20 minutes on foot westward === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みすししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[JA:水主神社 (城陽市)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153262}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Mizushi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto.jpg|250px]]
Place of worship (honden in the back) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto}}
Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (major)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday in mid-November |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mizushi Shrine|position=top|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=180|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mizushi Shrine''' (みずしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined at Mizushi Miyababa, {{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}, and its former rank was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === According to shrine tradition, the ten deities currently enshrined are as follows: *{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|Q106241464|WD=Q106241464|lt=Amemurakumo-no-Kami}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Mikoto|Q97613650|WD=Q97613650|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Kami}} *Take-Nukashi-Aka-no-Mikoto *Take-Tsutsukusa-no-Mikoto *Take-Tase-no-Mikoto *Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto *Yamatotama-hiko-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto However, according to the "National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals," the enshrined deities are the following five: *Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami *Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto *Oonui-no-Mikoto *Konui-no-Mikoto According to the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, the entry indicates ten seats at Mizushi Shrine and notes participation of two deities in the {{ill|lt=Ainame Festival|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944}}, showing that there were originally ten deities, two of which were Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto. === History === === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, in the reign of Emperor Sujin, Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto moved {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} to Yamato Kasanui no Mura, and Nunakiiri-hime-no-Mikoto was made to enshrine Yamato Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamato and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamashiro; this shrine is identified with the Yamashiro enshrinement. Since ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' ({{ill|lt=815|Q29917|WD=Q29917}}) states "Yamashiro no kuni shinbetsu, {{ill|lt=Mizushi clan||WD=|lt=Mizushi no Atai}}, descendants of Hoakari-no-Mikoto," it is inferred that this shrine originally enshrined the clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari Myojin}} (Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami). === Overview === In {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2) rain-prayer rites were held, and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 1) prayers for wind and rain were performed. National histories repeatedly record offerings for rain prayer at this shrine, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Rinji-sai|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499}} it was selected among the eighty-five rain-prayer deities, indicating a strong reputation for efficacy in rain prayer. In the Engishiki Jinmyocho it is ranked as a major shrine. === Divine ranks === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" *{{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872}}) {{ill|lt=November 20|Q3024|WD=Q3024}}, promoted from Junior Fourth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Upper (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" === Precincts === The area beyond the worship hall is protected by iron bars; since the main hall and precinct shrines are all inside, visitors cannot approach directly in front of the main hall. The Mizushi Shrine main hall stands in front, with Kabaitsuki Shrine (below) on the left and Koromonui Shrine on the right. ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Honden.jpg|Main hall ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Haiden.jpg|Worship hall ファイル:Koromonui Jinja Mizushi.jpg|Koromonui Shrine === Kabaitsuki Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Kabaitsuki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Kabaitsuki Jinja Mizushi.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{Coord|34|51|17|N|135|47|22.4|E|region:JP-26|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (major) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=February 20|Q2344|WD=Q2344}} |神事 = |地図 = }} '''Kabaitsuki Shrine''' (かばいづきじんじゃ) is a precinct shrine of Mizushi Shrine. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}. ==== History ==== Its founding date is unknown. In the same {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678}}, there is the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine (Kyotanabe)|Q54153263|WD=Q54153263|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine}}, and a relationship has been pointed out. It is also said that this shrine formerly stood in Osumi together with Tsukuyomi Shrine. However, due to flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kizu River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River}}, the shrine buildings were washed away, and in {{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 12) it was relocated into the precincts of Mizushi Shrine. Afterward it came under shared administration with Mizushi Shrine. ==== Divine rank ==== *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabaitsuki no Kami" === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kinki Nippon Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kyoto Line}} {{ill|lt=Tonominosho Station|Q7821630|WD=Q7821630}}; then 20 minutes on foot westward === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みすししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Since ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' ({{ill|lt=815|Q29917|WD=Q29917}}) states "Yamashiro no kuni shinbetsu, {{ill|lt=Mizushi clan|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Mizushi no Atai}}, descendants of Hoakari-no-Mikoto," it is inferred that this shrine originally enshrined the clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari Myojin}} (Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami). === Overview === In {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2) rain-prayer rites were held, and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 1) prayers for wind and rain were performed. National histories repeatedly record offerings for rain prayer at this shrine, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Rinji-sai|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499}} it was selected among the eighty-five rain-prayer deities, indicating a strong reputation for efficacy in rain prayer. In the Engishiki Jinmyocho it is ranked as a major shrine. === Divine ranks === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" *{{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872}}) {{ill|lt=November 20|Q3024|WD=Q3024}}, promoted from Junior Fourth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Upper (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" === Precincts === The area beyond the worship hall is protected by iron bars; since the main hall and precinct shrines are all inside, visitors cannot approach directly in front of the main hall. The Mizushi Shrine main hall stands in front, with Kabaitsuki Shrine (below) on the left and Koromonui Shrine on the right. ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Honden.jpg|Main hall ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Haiden.jpg|Worship hall ファイル:Koromonui Jinja Mizushi.jpg|Koromonui Shrine === Kabaitsuki Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Kabaitsuki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Kabaitsuki Jinja Mizushi.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{Coord|34|51|17|N|135|47|22.4|E|region:JP-26|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (major) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=February 20|Q2344|WD=Q2344}} |神事 = |地図 = }} '''Kabaitsuki Shrine''' (かばいづきじんじゃ) is a precinct shrine of Mizushi Shrine. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}. ==== History ==== Its founding date is unknown. In the same {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678}}, there is the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine (Kyotanabe)|Q54153263|WD=Q54153263|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine}}, and a relationship has been pointed out. It is also said that this shrine formerly stood in Osumi together with Tsukuyomi Shrine. However, due to flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kizu River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River}}, the shrine buildings were washed away, and in {{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 12) it was relocated into the precincts of Mizushi Shrine. Afterward it came under shared administration with Mizushi Shrine. ==== Divine rank ==== *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabaitsuki no Kami" === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kinki Nippon Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kyoto Line}} {{ill|lt=Tonominosho Station|Q7821630|WD=Q7821630}}; then 20 minutes on foot westward === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みすししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[JA:水主神社 (城陽市)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153262}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Mizushi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto.jpg|250px]]
Place of worship (honden in the back) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto}}
Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (major)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday in mid-November |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mizushi Shrine|position=top|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=180|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mizushi Shrine''' (みずしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined at Mizushi Miyababa, {{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}, and its former rank was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === According to shrine tradition, the ten deities currently enshrined are as follows: *{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|Q106241464|WD=Q106241464|lt=Amemurakumo-no-Kami}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Mikoto|Q97613650|WD=Q97613650|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Kami}} *Take-Nukashi-Aka-no-Mikoto *Take-Tsutsukusa-no-Mikoto *Take-Tase-no-Mikoto *Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto *Yamatotama-hiko-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto However, according to the "National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals," the enshrined deities are the following five: *Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami *Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto *Oonui-no-Mikoto *Konui-no-Mikoto According to the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, the entry indicates ten seats at Mizushi Shrine and notes participation of two deities in the {{ill|lt=Ainame Festival|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944}}, showing that there were originally ten deities, two of which were Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto. === History === === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, in the reign of Emperor Sujin, Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto moved {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} to Yamato Kasanui no Mura, and Nunakiiri-hime-no-Mikoto was made to enshrine Yamato Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamato and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamashiro; this shrine is identified with the Yamashiro enshrinement. Since ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' ({{ill|lt=815|Q29917|WD=Q29917}}) states "Yamashiro no kuni shinbetsu, {{ill|lt=Mizushi clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizushi no Atai}}, descendants of Hoakari-no-Mikoto," it is inferred that this shrine originally enshrined the clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari Myojin}} (Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami). === Overview === In {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2) rain-prayer rites were held, and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 1) prayers for wind and rain were performed. National histories repeatedly record offerings for rain prayer at this shrine, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Rinji-sai|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499}} it was selected among the eighty-five rain-prayer deities, indicating a strong reputation for efficacy in rain prayer. In the Engishiki Jinmyocho it is ranked as a major shrine. === Divine ranks === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" *{{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872}}) {{ill|lt=November 20|Q3024|WD=Q3024}}, promoted from Junior Fourth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Upper (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" === Precincts === The area beyond the worship hall is protected by iron bars; since the main hall and precinct shrines are all inside, visitors cannot approach directly in front of the main hall. The Mizushi Shrine main hall stands in front, with Kabaitsuki Shrine (below) on the left and Koromonui Shrine on the right. ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Honden.jpg|Main hall ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Haiden.jpg|Worship hall ファイル:Koromonui Jinja Mizushi.jpg|Koromonui Shrine === Kabaitsuki Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Kabaitsuki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Kabaitsuki Jinja Mizushi.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{Coord|34|51|17|N|135|47|22.4|E|region:JP-26|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (major) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=February 20|Q2344|WD=Q2344}} |神事 = |地図 = }} '''Kabaitsuki Shrine''' (かばいづきじんじゃ) is a precinct shrine of Mizushi Shrine. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}. ==== History ==== Its founding date is unknown. In the same {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678}}, there is the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine (Kyotanabe)|Q54153263|WD=Q54153263|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine}}, and a relationship has been pointed out. It is also said that this shrine formerly stood in Osumi together with Tsukuyomi Shrine. However, due to flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kizu River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River}}, the shrine buildings were washed away, and in {{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 12) it was relocated into the precincts of Mizushi Shrine. Afterward it came under shared administration with Mizushi Shrine. ==== Divine rank ==== *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabaitsuki no Kami" === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kinki Nippon Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kyoto Line}} {{ill|lt=Tonominosho Station|Q7821630|WD=Q7821630}}; then 20 minutes on foot westward === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みすししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[JA:水主神社 (城陽市)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153262}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Mizushi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto.jpg|250px]]
Place of worship (honden in the back) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto}}
Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday in mid-November |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mizushi Shrine|position=top|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=180|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mizushi Shrine''' (みずしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined at Mizushi Miyababa, {{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}, and its former rank was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === According to shrine tradition, the ten deities currently enshrined are as follows: *{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|Q106241464|WD=Q106241464|lt=Amemurakumo-no-Kami}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Mikoto|Q97613650|WD=Q97613650|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Kami}} *Take-Nukashi-Aka-no-Mikoto *Take-Tsutsukusa-no-Mikoto *Take-Tase-no-Mikoto *Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto *Yamatotama-hiko-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto However, according to the "National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals," the enshrined deities are the following five: *Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami *Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto *Oonui-no-Mikoto *Konui-no-Mikoto According to the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, the entry indicates ten seats at Mizushi Shrine and notes participation of two deities in the {{ill|lt=Ainame Festival|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944}}, showing that there were originally ten deities, two of which were Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto. === History === === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, in the reign of Emperor Sujin, Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto moved {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} to Yamato Kasanui no Mura, and Nunakiiri-hime-no-Mikoto was made to enshrine Yamato Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamato and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamashiro; this shrine is identified with the Yamashiro enshrinement. Since ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' ({{ill|lt=815|Q29917|WD=Q29917}}) states "Yamashiro no kuni shinbetsu, {{ill|lt=Mizushi clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizushi no Atai}}, descendants of Hoakari-no-Mikoto," it is inferred that this shrine originally enshrined the clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari Myojin}} (Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami). === Overview === In {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2) rain-prayer rites were held, and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 1) prayers for wind and rain were performed. National histories repeatedly record offerings for rain prayer at this shrine, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Rinji-sai|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499}} it was selected among the eighty-five rain-prayer deities, indicating a strong reputation for efficacy in rain prayer. In the Engishiki Jinmyocho it is ranked as a major shrine. === Divine ranks === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" *{{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872}}) {{ill|lt=November 20|Q3024|WD=Q3024}}, promoted from Junior Fourth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Upper (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" === Precincts === The area beyond the worship hall is protected by iron bars; since the main hall and precinct shrines are all inside, visitors cannot approach directly in front of the main hall. The Mizushi Shrine main hall stands in front, with Kabaitsuki Shrine (below) on the left and Koromonui Shrine on the right. ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Honden.jpg|Main hall ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Haiden.jpg|Worship hall ファイル:Koromonui Jinja Mizushi.jpg|Koromonui Shrine === Kabaitsuki Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Kabaitsuki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Kabaitsuki Jinja Mizushi.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{Coord|34|51|17|N|135|47|22.4|E|region:JP-26|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (major) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=February 20|Q2344|WD=Q2344}} |神事 = |地図 = }} '''Kabaitsuki Shrine''' (かばいづきじんじゃ) is a precinct shrine of Mizushi Shrine. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}. ==== History ==== Its founding date is unknown. In the same {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678}}, there is the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine (Kyotanabe)|Q54153263|WD=Q54153263|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine}}, and a relationship has been pointed out. It is also said that this shrine formerly stood in Osumi together with Tsukuyomi Shrine. However, due to flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kizu River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River}}, the shrine buildings were washed away, and in {{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 12) it was relocated into the precincts of Mizushi Shrine. Afterward it came under shared administration with Mizushi Shrine. ==== Divine rank ==== *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabaitsuki no Kami" === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kinki Nippon Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kyoto Line}} {{ill|lt=Tonominosho Station|Q7821630|WD=Q7821630}}; then 20 minutes on foot westward === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みすししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is also said that this shrine formerly stood in Osumi together with Tsukuyomi Shrine. However, due to flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kizu River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River}}, the shrine buildings were washed away, and in {{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 12) it was relocated into the precincts of Mizushi Shrine. Afterward it came under shared administration with Mizushi Shrine. ==== Divine rank ==== *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabaitsuki no Kami" === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kinki Nippon Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kyoto Line}} {{ill|lt=Tonominosho Station|Q7821630|WD=Q7821630}}; then 20 minutes on foot westward === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みすししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[JA:水主神社 (城陽市)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153262}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Mizushi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto.jpg|250px]]
Place of worship (honden in the back) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto}}
Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday in mid-November |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mizushi Shrine|position=top|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=180|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mizushi Shrine''' (みずしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined at Mizushi Miyababa, {{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}, and its former rank was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === According to shrine tradition, the ten deities currently enshrined are as follows: *{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|Q106241464|WD=Q106241464|lt=Amemurakumo-no-Kami}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Mikoto|Q97613650|WD=Q97613650|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Kami}} *Take-Nukashi-Aka-no-Mikoto *Take-Tsutsukusa-no-Mikoto *Take-Tase-no-Mikoto *Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto *Yamatotama-hiko-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto However, according to the "National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals," the enshrined deities are the following five: *Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami *Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto *Oonui-no-Mikoto *Konui-no-Mikoto According to the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, the entry indicates ten seats at Mizushi Shrine and notes participation of two deities in the {{ill|lt=Ainame Festival|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944}}, showing that there were originally ten deities, two of which were Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto. === History === === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, in the reign of Emperor Sujin, Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto moved {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} to Yamato Kasanui no Mura, and Nunakiiri-hime-no-Mikoto was made to enshrine Yamato Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamato and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamashiro; this shrine is identified with the Yamashiro enshrinement. Since ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' ({{ill|lt=815|Q29917|WD=Q29917}}) states "Yamashiro no kuni shinbetsu, {{ill|lt=Mizushi clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizushi no Atai}}, descendants of Hoakari-no-Mikoto," it is inferred that this shrine originally enshrined the clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari Myojin}} (Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami). === Overview === In {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2) rain-prayer rites were held, and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 1) prayers for wind and rain were performed. National histories repeatedly record offerings for rain prayer at this shrine, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Rinji-sai|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499}} it was selected among the eighty-five rain-prayer deities, indicating a strong reputation for efficacy in rain prayer. In the Engishiki Jinmyocho it is ranked as a major shrine. === Divine ranks === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" *{{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872}}) {{ill|lt=November 20|Q3024|WD=Q3024}}, promoted from Junior Fourth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Upper (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" === Precincts === The area beyond the worship hall is protected by iron bars; since the main hall and precinct shrines are all inside, visitors cannot approach directly in front of the main hall. The Mizushi Shrine main hall stands in front, with Kabaitsuki Shrine (below) on the left and Koromonui Shrine on the right. ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Honden.jpg|Main hall ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Haiden.jpg|Worship hall ファイル:Koromonui Jinja Mizushi.jpg|Koromonui Shrine === Kabaitsuki Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Kabaitsuki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Kabaitsuki Jinja Mizushi.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{Coord|34|51|17|N|135|47|22.4|E|region:JP-26|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (major) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=February 20|Q2344|WD=Q2344}} |神事 = |地図 = }} '''Kabaitsuki Shrine''' (かばいづきじんじゃ) is a precinct shrine of Mizushi Shrine. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}. ==== History ==== Its founding date is unknown. In the same {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678}}, there is the shikinaisha {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine (Kyotanabe)|Q54153263|WD=Q54153263|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine}}, and a relationship has been pointed out. It is also said that this shrine formerly stood in Osumi together with Tsukuyomi Shrine. However, due to flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kizu River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River}}, the shrine buildings were washed away, and in {{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 12) it was relocated into the precincts of Mizushi Shrine. Afterward it came under shared administration with Mizushi Shrine. ==== Divine rank ==== *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabaitsuki no Kami" === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kinki Nippon Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kyoto Line}} {{ill|lt=Tonominosho Station|Q7821630|WD=Q7821630}}; then 20 minutes on foot westward === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みすししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday in mid-November |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mizushi Shrine|position=top|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=180|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mizushi Shrine''' (みずしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined at Mizushi Miyababa, {{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former rank was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === According to shrine tradition, the ten deities currently enshrined are as follows: *{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami|1=Nigihayahi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|Q106241464|WD=Q106241464|lt=Amemurakumo-no-Kami|1=Ame no Murakumo}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Mikoto|Q97613650|WD=Q97613650|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Kami|1=Amaoshio no mikoto}} *Take-Nukashi-Aka-no-Mikoto *Take-Tsutsukusa-no-Mikoto *Take-Tase-no-Mikoto *Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto *Yamatotama-hiko-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto However, according to the "National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals," the enshrined deities are the following five: *Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami *Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto *Oonui-no-Mikoto *Konui-no-Mikoto According to the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, the entry indicates ten seats at Mizushi Shrine and notes participation of two deities in the {{ill|lt=Ainame Festival|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944|1=Ainame Festival}}, showing that there were originally ten deities, two of which were Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto. === History === === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, in the reign of Emperor Sujin, Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto moved {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} to Yamato Kasanui no Mura, and Nunakiiri-hime-no-Mikoto was made to enshrine Yamato Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamato and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamashiro; this shrine is identified with the Yamashiro enshrinement. Since ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' ({{ill|lt=815|Q29917|WD=Q29917|1=815}}) states "Yamashiro no kuni shinbetsu, {{ill|lt=Mizushi clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizushi no Atai}}, descendants of Hoakari-no-Mikoto," it is inferred that this shrine originally enshrined the clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari Myojin|1=Amenohoakari}} (Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami). === Overview === In {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2) rain-prayer rites were held, and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 1) prayers for wind and rain were performed. National histories repeatedly record offerings for rain prayer at this shrine, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Rinji-sai|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499|1=Rinji-sai}} it was selected among the eighty-five rain-prayer deities, indicating a strong reputation for efficacy in rain prayer. In the Engishiki Jinmyocho it is ranked as a major shrine. === Divine ranks === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" *{{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872|1=866}}) {{ill|lt=November 20|Q3024|WD=Q3024|1=November 20}}, promoted from Junior Fourth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Upper (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" === Precincts === The area beyond the worship hall is protected by iron bars; since the main hall and precinct shrines are all inside, visitors cannot approach directly in front of the main hall. The Mizushi Shrine main hall stands in front, with Kabaitsuki Shrine (below) on the left and Koromonui Shrine on the right. ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Honden.jpg|Main hall ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Haiden.jpg|Worship hall ファイル:Koromonui Jinja Mizushi.jpg|Koromonui Shrine === Kabaitsuki Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Kabaitsuki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Kabaitsuki Jinja Mizushi.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{Coord|34|51|17|N|135|47|22.4|E|region:JP-26|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=February 20|Q2344|WD=Q2344|1=February 20}} |神事 = |地図 = }} '''Kabaitsuki Shrine''' (かばいづきじんじゃ) is a precinct shrine of Mizushi Shrine. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}. ==== History ==== Its founding date is unknown. In the same {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, there is the shikinaisha {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine (Kyotanabe)|Q54153263|WD=Q54153263|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, and a relationship has been pointed out. It is also said that this shrine formerly stood in Osumi together with Tsukuyomi Shrine. However, due to flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kizu River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River|1=Kizu River}}, the shrine buildings were washed away, and in {{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614|1=1672}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 12) it was relocated into the precincts of Mizushi Shrine. Afterward it came under shared administration with Mizushi Shrine. ==== Divine rank ==== *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kabaitsuki no Kami" === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kinki Nippon Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}} {{ill|lt=Tonominosho Station|Q7821630|WD=Q7821630|1=Tonoshō Station}}; then 20 minutes on foot westward === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みすししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In the same {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, there is the shikinaisha {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine (Kyotanabe)|Q54153263|WD=Q54153263|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, and a relationship has been pointed out. It is also said that this shrine formerly stood in Osumi together with Tsukuyomi Shrine. However, due to flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kizu River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River|1=Kizu River}}, the shrine buildings were washed away, and in {{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614|1=1672}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 12) it was relocated into the precincts of Mizushi Shrine. Afterward it came under shared administration with Mizushi Shrine. ==== Divine rank ==== *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kabaitsuki no Kami" === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kinki Nippon Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}} {{ill|lt=Tonominosho Station|Q7821630|WD=Q7821630|1=Tonoshō Station}}; then 20 minutes on foot westward === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みすししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]][[Category:qq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府城陽市水主宮馬場1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Mizushi-Jinja, Joyo}} [[JA:水主神社 (城陽市)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153262}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Mizushi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto.jpg|250px]]
Place of worship (honden in the back) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami|1=Nigihayahi}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}}
Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday in mid-November |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mizushi Shrine|position=top|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=180|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mizushi Shrine''' (みずしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined at Mizushi Miyababa, {{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former rank was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === According to shrine tradition, the ten deities currently enshrined are as follows: *{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami|1=Nigihayahi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|Q106241464|WD=Q106241464|lt=Amemurakumo-no-Kami|1=Ame no Murakumo}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Mikoto|Q97613650|WD=Q97613650|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Kami|1=Amaoshio no mikoto}} *Take-Nukashi-Aka-no-Mikoto *Take-Tsutsukusa-no-Mikoto *Take-Tase-no-Mikoto *Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto *Yamatotama-hiko-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto However, according to the "National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals," the enshrined deities are the following five: *Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami *Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto *Oonui-no-Mikoto *Konui-no-Mikoto According to the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, the entry indicates ten seats at Mizushi Shrine and notes participation of two deities in the {{ill|lt=Ainame Festival|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944|1=Ainame Festival}}, showing that there were originally ten deities, two of which were Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto. === History === === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, in the reign of Emperor Sujin, Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto moved {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} to Yamato Kasanui no Mura, and Nunakiiri-hime-no-Mikoto was made to enshrine Yamato Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamato and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamashiro; this shrine is identified with the Yamashiro enshrinement. Since ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' ({{ill|lt=815|Q29917|WD=Q29917|1=815}}) states "Yamashiro no kuni shinbetsu, {{ill|lt=Mizushi clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizushi no Atai}}, descendants of Hoakari-no-Mikoto," it is inferred that this shrine originally enshrined the clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari Myojin|1=Amenohoakari}} (Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami). === Overview === In {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2) rain-prayer rites were held, and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 1) prayers for wind and rain were performed. National histories repeatedly record offerings for rain prayer at this shrine, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Rinji-sai|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499|1=Rinji-sai}} it was selected among the eighty-five rain-prayer deities, indicating a strong reputation for efficacy in rain prayer. In the Engishiki Jinmyocho it is ranked as a major shrine. === Divine ranks === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" *{{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872|1=866}}) {{ill|lt=November 20|Q3024|WD=Q3024|1=November 20}}, promoted from Junior Fourth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Upper (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" === Precincts === The area beyond the worship hall is protected by iron bars; since the main hall and precinct shrines are all inside, visitors cannot approach directly in front of the main hall. The Mizushi Shrine main hall stands in front, with Kabaitsuki Shrine (below) on the left and Koromonui Shrine on the right. ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Honden.jpg|Main hall ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Haiden.jpg|Worship hall ファイル:Koromonui Jinja Mizushi.jpg|Koromonui Shrine === Kabaitsuki Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Kabaitsuki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Kabaitsuki Jinja Mizushi.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{Coord|34|51|17|N|135|47|22.4|E|region:JP-26|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=February 20|Q2344|WD=Q2344|1=February 20}} |神事 = |地図 = }} '''Kabaitsuki Shrine''' (かばいづきじんじゃ) is a precinct shrine of Mizushi Shrine. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}. ==== History ==== Its founding date is unknown. In the same {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, there is the shikinaisha {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine (Kyotanabe)|Q54153263|WD=Q54153263|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, and a relationship has been pointed out. It is also said that this shrine formerly stood in Osumi together with Tsukuyomi Shrine. However, due to flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kizu River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River|1=Kizu River}}, the shrine buildings were washed away, and in {{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614|1=1672}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 12) it was relocated into the precincts of Mizushi Shrine. Afterward it came under shared administration with Mizushi Shrine. ==== Divine rank ==== *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kabaitsuki no Kami" === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kinki Nippon Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}} {{ill|lt=Tonominosho Station|Q7821630|WD=Q7821630|1=Tonoshō Station}}; then 20 minutes on foot westward === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みすししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]][[Category:qq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府城陽市水主宮馬場1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Mizushi-Jinja, Joyo}} [[JA:水主神社 (城陽市)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153262}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Mizushi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto.jpg|250px]]
Place of worship (honden in the back) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami|1=Nigihayahi}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}}
Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday in mid-November |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mizushi Shrine|position=top|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=180|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mizushi Shrine''' (みずしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined at Mizushi Miyababa, {{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former rank was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === According to shrine tradition, the ten deities currently enshrined are as follows: *{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami|1=Nigihayahi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|Q106241464|WD=Q106241464|lt=Amemurakumo-no-Kami|1=Ame no Murakumo}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Mikoto|Q97613650|WD=Q97613650|lt=Ame-no-Oshio-no-Kami|1=Amaoshio no mikoto}} *Take-Nukashi-Aka-no-Mikoto *Take-Tsutsukusa-no-Mikoto *Take-Tase-no-Mikoto *Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto *Yamatotama-hiko-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto However, according to the "National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals," the enshrined deities are the following five: *Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami *Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto *Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto *Oonui-no-Mikoto *Konui-no-Mikoto According to the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, the entry indicates ten seats at Mizushi Shrine and notes participation of two deities in the {{ill|lt=Ainame Festival|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944|1=Ainame Festival}}, showing that there were originally ten deities, two of which were Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto. === History === === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, in the reign of Emperor Sujin, Toyosukiiri-hime-no-Mikoto moved {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} to Yamato Kasanui no Mura, and Nunakiiri-hime-no-Mikoto was made to enshrine Yamato Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamato and Yamashiro Okunitama-no-Mikoto in Yamashiro; this shrine is identified with the Yamashiro enshrinement. Since ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' ({{ill|lt=815|Q29917|WD=Q29917|1=815}}) states "Yamashiro no kuni shinbetsu, {{ill|lt=Mizushi clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizushi no Atai}}, descendants of Hoakari-no-Mikoto," it is inferred that this shrine originally enshrined the clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari Myojin|1=Amenohoakari}} (Amateru-Mi-Tama-no-Kami). === Overview === In {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2) rain-prayer rites were held, and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 1) prayers for wind and rain were performed. National histories repeatedly record offerings for rain prayer at this shrine, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Rinji-sai|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499|1=Rinji-sai}} it was selected among the eighty-five rain-prayer deities, indicating a strong reputation for efficacy in rain prayer. In the Engishiki Jinmyocho it is ranked as a major shrine. === Divine ranks === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" *{{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872|1=866}}) {{ill|lt=November 20|Q3024|WD=Q3024|1=November 20}}, promoted from Junior Fourth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Upper (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Mizushi no Kami" === Precincts === The area beyond the worship hall is protected by iron bars; since the main hall and precinct shrines are all inside, visitors cannot approach directly in front of the main hall. The Mizushi Shrine main hall stands in front, with Kabaitsuki Shrine (below) on the left and Koromonui Shrine on the right. ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Honden.jpg|Main hall ファイル:Mizushi Jinja Kyoto Haiden.jpg|Worship hall ファイル:Koromonui Jinja Mizushi.jpg|Koromonui Shrine === Kabaitsuki Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Kabaitsuki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Kabaitsuki Jinja Mizushi.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 |位置 = {{Coord|34|51|17|N|135|47|22.4|E|region:JP-26|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=February 20|Q2344|WD=Q2344|1=February 20}} |神事 = |地図 = }} '''Kabaitsuki Shrine''' (かばいづきじんじゃ) is a precinct shrine of Mizushi Shrine. It is a {{ill|lt=major shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}. ==== History ==== Its founding date is unknown. In the same {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, there is the shikinaisha {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine (Kyotanabe)|Q54153263|WD=Q54153263|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, and a relationship has been pointed out. It is also said that this shrine formerly stood in Osumi together with Tsukuyomi Shrine. However, due to flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kizu River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River|1=Kizu River}}, the shrine buildings were washed away, and in {{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614|1=1672}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 12) it was relocated into the precincts of Mizushi Shrine. Afterward it came under shared administration with Mizushi Shrine. ==== Divine rank ==== *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kabaitsuki no Kami" === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}Mizushi Miyababa 1 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kinki Nippon Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}} {{ill|lt=Tonominosho Station|Q7821630|WD=Q7821630|1=Tonoshō Station}}; then 20 minutes on foot westward === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mizushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]][[Category:qq]]
